JD Vance has pushed back against claims that the US is interfering in Hungarian politics, describing the accusations as “darkly ironic”, as a set of polls suggested the opposition Tisza party could win a supermajority in the forthcoming elections. Until they know for certain what happened, they will not disclose any details about the explosives found near the Turkish Stream pipeline.

stories.title: Carnival celebrations at a Hungarian retirement home: János Bődey’s best photograph
stories.summary: ‘I asked them the secret to a happy life. “Good genes,” replied Magdolna, who was 87. “Avoid borrowing money,” added Irén, 86’ When I saw these two elderly ladies dressed as bride and groom in a retirement home in Páty, near Budapest, my first thought was to wonder why they had chosen those particular costumes. I took their picture in the home’s lounge and afterwards we had a long conversation. I asked them about their lives, what they believed was the secret to a long life, and how to preserve

stories.title: JD Vance criticizes Zelenskyy and Ukraine in Hungary on visit campaigning for Viktor Orban
stories.summary: US Vice President JD Vance has criticized EU leaders and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy during his visit to Hungary, echoing Prime Minister Viktor Orban's campaign messaging. The Kremlin chimed in from Moscow, too.

stories.title: Virtual Romanian singer goes viral but sparks criticism
stories.summary: In Romania, an AI-generated singer inspired by poems about the Roma community has become an overnight success. But critics say her creator is monetizing the woes of a marginalized group without involving its members.

stories.title: Hungarians living in Ukraine caught between front lines

stories.title: Timor-Leste is vulnerable to ‘infiltration by foreign organized crime’, president José Ramos-Horta says
stories.summary: Australian federal police say they are working with tiny nation to respond to threat of online scam centres Timor-Leste is vulnerable to “infiltration by foreign organized crime”, the country’s president, José Ramos-Horta, has warned. His comments come as Australian federal police confirmed to the Guardian the force is providing support to local law enforcement in Timor-Leste, including a December 2025 visit from the agency’s digital forensic and cyber experts. stories.title: Russian GRU exploited vulnerable routers worldwide to steal sensitive information, probe reveals
stories.summary: An international investigation revealed how Russia’s GRU cyber unit, known as "Fancy Bear," managed to steal sensitive information from governments and the military using badly protected routers.

stories.title: Trump’s Iran ceasefire doesn’t mean Europe’s economy is out of the danger zone
stories.summary: Don’t be popping those champagne corks yet: Europe’s economy will still be hurting from the war in the Middle East for months — and maybe longer. Financial markets rallied exuberantly on Wednesday in relief at the announcement of a two-week ceasefire by the U.S. and Iran overnight. Investors and traders rushed to tone down expectations of a nightmare scenario in which spiking energy prices would crush growth and stoke higher inflation. The euro and pound both leaped versus the dollar, while bond

stories.title: French MEP Rima Hassan files lawsuit over leaks following arrest
stories.summary: PARIS — French Member of the European Parliament Rima Hassan has filed a complaint after details of her arrest were leaked to the media while she was in custody last week. Hassan, a member of The Left group in the European Parliament, was taken into police custody after posting a quote on X by far-left Japanese militant Kōzō Okamoto, who was convicted of a terrorist attack that killed 26 people in Israel in 1972. The MEP, who was born in a Palestinian refugee camp in Syria, has placed pro-Palest

stories.title: Trump threatens 50 percent tariffs on Iran arms supplies. His legal path is murky.
stories.summary: President Donald Trump on Wednesday threatened 50 percent tariffs on any country that supplies military weapons to Iran, though it’s not clear he has the legal authority to do so. “A Country supplying Military Weapons to Iran will be immediately tariffed, on any and all goods sold to the United States of America, 50%, effective immediately. There will be no exclusions or exemptions!” Trump  wrote in a post on Truth Social . While the president previously wielded tariffs to pressure other co

stories.title: Israel greets Iran ceasefire with more unease than relief
stories.summary: TEL AVIV, Israel — Israel greeted the Iran ceasefire with more unease than relief, as overnight missile fire, sharp political backlash and deep skepticism about Tehran’s intentions reinforced a broad sense that the conflict is paused, but far from resolved. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu faced swift criticism from political opponents on the left and right, who slammed the Israeli premier for failing to eliminate the core threats posed by Iran’s nuclear program, missile arsenal and regional pr

stories.title: IDF conduct in Lebanon plunges Israel-Europe relations to lower depths
stories.summary: Spain and Italy have demanded explanations from Israel after the country’s soldiers in Lebanon detained a Spanish United Nations peacekeeper and fired warning shots at an Italian convoy. The incidents mark another low point in Israel’s relations with Europe, already severely damaged by the war in Gaza and now further strained by the country’s ongoing military campaign in Lebanon. stories.title: Europe fears getting stuck with the bill after Trump’s Iran deal
stories.summary: BRUSSELS ― Donald Trump makes a mess. Europe gets called on to clean it up, or at least pay a hefty price to deal with it. That’s a pattern the EU fears is about to repeat itself in the Strait of Hormuz after the U.S. president announced a ceasefire agreement with Iran, five EU diplomats and officials said. stories.summary: President Donald Trump is sending Vice President JD Vance and other top negotiators to Pakistan for mediated talks with Iran this weekend aimed at cementing a permanent ceasefire, the White House announced on Wednesday. stories.title: Italy’s Giorgia Meloni attempts political reset after referendum defeat
stories.summary: ROME — Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni will try on Thursday to revamp her premiership, after rising energy prices and her ties to U.S. President Donald Trump helped contribute to a bruising referendum defeat last month. “The war created uncertainty, and a sense of fear has prevailed among those alarmed by the rise in petrol prices,” said Paolo Barelli, whip for the center-right Forza Italia party and member of the coalition government, after the electoral loss . stories.title: Iran's defence-industrial base 'completely' destroyed, US defence secretary says

stories.title: EU Parliament’s agriculture committee greenlights ‘veggie bacon’ ban
stories.summary: The European Parliament’s AGRI committee voted to support the deal struck by co-legislators on targeted amendments to the EU’s Common Market Organisation rules for agricultural products. The rule changes target contracts primarily, with a view to strengthening farmers’ position in the supply chain. But the trilogue compromise also included a ban on using several meat-related terms to advertise plant-based products or lab-grown meat, which has largely overshadowed the technical

stories.title: Truck drivers warn fuel crisis could see industry ‘grind to a halt’ – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Cyclone-hammered reefs can take many years to recover, study finds Storm-ravaged coral reefs might never have the years required to recover if tropical cyclones become more intense and frequent due to climate change, marine researchers say. stories.title: ‘I want to make myself obsolete’: the MP fighting for Greenland’s self-governance
stories.summary: Qarsoq Høegh-Dam aims to use his seat in Danish parliament to shift power from Copenhagen to Nuuk It’s not the standard motto for a newly elected parliamentarian, but Qarsoq Høegh-Dam is adamant: if he does his job properly, there will soon be no need for it. “I want to make myself as obsolete as possible,” he said. Last month, Høegh-Dam, a Greenlandic politician, became the first member of the pro-independence Naleraq to be elected to the Danish parliament. The new MP is clear that if all goes

stories.title: Family pay tribute to British teenager killed in motorcycle crash in Vietnam
stories.summary: Orla Wates, 19, who died after incident on popular Ha Giang loop, described as ‘beautiful, independent and very funny’ The family of a British teenager have paid tribute to their daughter who died after a motorcycle crash on a popular route in Vietnam. The incident occurred on the Ha Giang loop in the country’s north, and Orla Wates, 19, died at the Viet Duc university hospital in Hanoi, according to Viet Nam News. Continue reading...

stories.title: Chile’s far-right government rips up plan for memorial at Pinochet torture site
stories.summary: New administration reverses expropriation of property founded by ex-Nazi Paul Schäfer, leaving victims in limbo With its Germanic crosses and colourful toy-town facades, the village square of the tiny Chilean settlement of Villa Baviera gives little indication of the horrors of its past. Until 1991, this cattle town of a few hundred people was a compound known as Colonia Dignidad. Its leader, Paul Schäfer, a former Nazi and weapons smuggler, bought a swathe of land in the valley in 1961, eventua

stories.title: Cuts to NDIS to be focus of Labor’s quietly launched ‘razor gang’ ahead of May budget
stories.summary: Exclusive: Taskforce led by former Treasury official Anthea Long will advise on cost-cutting options for $52bn program Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Labor has quietly established a razor gang to drive budget savings in the national disability insurance scheme, as it works to further rein in costs ahead of next month’s federal budget. An NDIS Sustainability Taskforce was established within the health department earlier this year, with instructions from the federal g

stories.title: Greece announces social media ban for under-15s, citing anxiety and sleep problems
stories.summary: PM says ban will come into force in January if it is backed by parliament and calls for united action across EU Greece has announced a social media ban for under-15s from 1 January, with the country’s prime minister, Kyriakos Mitsotakis, citing rising anxiety, sleep problems and the addictive design of online platforms – although he acknowledged it may incur the wrath of some children. “We have decided to go ahead with a difficult but necessary measure: ban access to social media for children un

stories.title: UK weather: temperature tops 26C on one of hottest April days of past 80 years
stories.summary: High of 26.5C recorded in Kew Gardens in south-west London but rain and cooler conditions are on the way Temperatures soared in parts of the UK as the country recorded its warmest day of the year so far – and one of the hottest April days in the last 80 years. A high of 26.5C (79.7F) was recorded in Kew Gardens, in south-west London, on Wednesday, the Met Office said. It was the hottest day recorded in the first half of April since 1946, according to the forecaster. Continue reading...

stories.title: British computer scientist denies he is bitcoin developer Satoshi Nakamoto
stories.summary: New York Times report claims London-born Adam Back is creator of the cryptocurrency after comparing writings A British computer scientist has insisted he is not the elusive developer of bitcoin, after a report claimed to unmask him as its creator. A story in the New York Times details a years-long effort to unmask Satoshi Nakamoto, the mysterious author of the bitcoin white paper which laid the theoretical foundations for modern digital currencies. Continue reading...

stories.title: Britain breaks solar energy record twice as UK’s biggest solar farm gets approval
stories.summary: Record high set on Monday and raised on Tuesday, with 14.4GW of electricity generated in sunny spring weather Britain’s sunny spring weather powered the grid to new solar energy records on two consecutive days this week. Solar farms in England, Wales and Scotland generated 14.1GW of low-carbon electricity at lunchtime on Monday, surpassing the previous high of 14GW in July last year. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘It’s not AI, it’s real’: shock as RSPCA releases images of 250 dogs found at property
stories.summary: Dozens of dogs were found crammed into single living room space at property in undisclosed location in UK More than 250 dogs have been found at a property in scenes so shocking that the RSPCA was forced to deny allegations that the images were faked by artificial intelligence. The animal welfare charity said it took in 87 dogs from the property at an undisclosed location in the UK and the remainder went to the Dogs Trust, another charity. Continue reading...

stories.title: How Pakistan secured ‘biggest diplomatic win in years’ with Iran ceasefire
stories.summary: Analysts say Pakistani officials’ efforts led to breakthrough that has helped avert catastrophe, at least for now Middle East crisis – live updates Pakistan’s leaders had almost lost hope. After more than two weeks of frantic negotiations, phonecalls and diplomatic summits to try to end the US-Israeli war with Iran, it looked like the conflict might instead be escalating into Islamabad’s worst nightmare . In a cabinet meeting held at about 5pm on Tuesday, Pakistan’s prime minister, Shehbaz Shari

stories.title: Democrats renew push to curb Trump’s Iran war as calls to use 25th amendment mount
stories.summary: Over 70 Democratic lawmakers are calling for amendment to be invoked to remove Trump from office amid war Democratic party leaders have vowed to renew the effort to curb Donald Trump’s war in Iran next week after several days of escalating tactics that culminated in a temporary ceasefire on Tuesday evening. In recent months, several war powers resolutions have failed in Congress after a handful of Democrats continued to vote alongside Republicans. But Trump’s aggressive overtures this week – inc

stories.title: Senate Democrats will force war powers vote, says minority leader Chuck Schumer – US politics live
stories.summary: Schumer spoke after top House Democrat called for immediate vote on Donald Trump’s ‘reckless war of choice’ Sign up for the Breaking News US email Pete Hegseth repeated Donald Trump’s social media comments that Iran will cease uranium enrichment – a condition that Tehran has previously refused to budge on. “Any material they should not have, will be removed right now,” Hegseth said. “The president has been clear from the beginning, there will be no Iranian nuclear weapons.” Continue reading...

stories.title: Germans pining for Gerhard Schröder forget his errors and ties to Putin. The SPD needs a fresh approach | Katja Hoyer
stories.summary: The former chancellor was the chief architect of Germany’s dependence on Russian fossil fuels and cuts to defence spending. Both haunt the country today The former German chancellor, Gerhard Schröder, is enjoying a curious political revival. Not so long ago, his reputation seemed in tatters. In light of the full-scale invasion of Ukraine, many came to regard his longstanding ties to Russia and personal friendship with Vladimir Putin as self-serving. Fellow Social Democrats (SPD) tried to expel h

stories.title: 60 years after the Indonesian mass killings: Is the Cold War back?
stories.summary: Six decades after helping the Indonesian army crush one of the biggest communist parties in the world, the US is once again meddling in countries it considers a thorn in its flesh. Is history repeating itself?

stories.title: Should Western influencers promote Taliban‑run Afghanistan?
stories.summary: In the race for clicks, likes and followers, some travel content creators are being criticized for promoting Afghanistan while ignoring the harsh reality of Taliban rule.

stories.title: Taiwan opposition leader Cheng Li-wun makes rare China visit
stories.summary: During a visit to a memorial for Taiwan's founding father in Nanjing, KMT leader Cheng Li-wun called for "reconciliation and unity" with Beijing. Taiwan's ruling democrats say China needs to stop its aggression.

stories.title: India's digital census prompts fear of hidden agendas

stories.title: Tropical Cyclone Vaianu may bring life-threatening winds to New Zealand, forecasters warn
stories.summary: Category 3 cyclone is moving south of Fiji towards New Zealand, with winds at centre in excess of 150km/h Tropical Cyclone Vaianu forming in the Pacific could bring life-threatening winds and heavy rain to New Zealand later this week, forecasters have said, with strong wind watches issued for the entire North Island. The category 3 cyclone is moving south of Fiji towards New Zealand, with winds around the centre in excess of 150km/h, MetService said on Wednesday. Continue reading...

stories.summary: If the president’s first term didn’t inoculate the American body politic against tyranny, there is no guarantee that a second dose will work Donald Trump is a despot and the US is a democracy. These things can be true simultaneously but not indefinitely. There is now deadlock in the struggle between a president who would be king and a constitution drafted in repudiation of monarchy. stories.title: Afghanistan, Pakistan held 'useful' peace talks, Kabul says

stories.title: Pollster estimates strong two-thirds majority for Tisza

stories.title: Jogerősen is elítélték a pécsi Volvo-botrány vádlottjait, egyiküket bilincsben vitték el a tárgyalóteremből
stories.summary: Gazdasági csalásért és pénzmosásért jogerősen bűnösnek mondta ki a Pécsi Ítélőtábla a másod- és negyedrendű vádlottakat.

stories.title: A bíróság szerint alkalmatlan volt RFK Jr. jelöltje, ezért a miniszter átírta a vakcinabizottság alkalmassági elvárását
stories.summary: A bíró szerint 15 jelöltből csak hatnak volt releváns tapasztalata.

stories.title: „Nem megyünk sehova” – mondta az amerikai védelmi miniszter, aki szerint Iránnak ki kéne küldenie a postagalambjait
stories.summary: Pete Hegseth arról beszélt a Pentagon sajtótájékoztatóján, hogy a tűzszünet ellenére készenlétben marad az amerikai hadsereg Irán körül.

stories.title: T. Danny: Összeesett az orrom pár nap leforgása alatt
stories.summary: Közhelyes marad, de itt-ott betalál: T. Danny dokusorozatának zárása már nem kerüli ki a lényeget, a tagadást és a visszafordíthatatlan következményeket.

stories.title: A Telex történetének eddigi legnagyobb választási műsorával készülünk április 12-re
stories.summary: Részvételi adatok, helyszíni bejelentkezések, élő elemzések, térkép és egy exkluzív kutatási adat, amelyet urnazáráskor fogunk publikálni. Érdemes lesz nálunk követni a választás napját!

stories.title: A legjobbak azok a járványok, amik soha nem történnek meg
stories.summary: A felújított pécsi Nemzeti Virológiai Laboratóriumban jártunk, megnéztük, hol kutatják a tudósok a legveszélyesebb vírusokat. A labor munkája nem látványos, mégis létfontosságú: itt készülnek fel a járványokra, amelyek talán soha nem következnek be.

stories.title: A veszélyeztetett gyerekek többsége láthatatlan a rendszernek
stories.summary: Egy hónapos munkáját tette közzé a Falakon Túl Munkacsoport, ami a zárt intézetekben bántalmazott gyerekek jövőjéért küzd. A szakemberek konkrét javaslatokat is megfogalmaztak. Szerintük sokszor még a dolgozók is úgy látják: nincs értelme jelezni a bántalmazást, úgysem történik semmi.

stories.title: Esélytelen, hogy az olajárak visszatérjenek a háború előtti szintre
stories.summary: Egyes elemzők szerint még ahhoz is nagy szerencse kell, hogy pár héten belül ne induljon újra a háború Irán, Izrael és az Egyesült Államok között.

stories.title: Hétnapos munkahetek, visszatartott fizetések, túlóra nincs – kínai vendégmunkásokkal készült jelentés a szegedi BYD-építkezés körülményeiről
stories.summary: Egy amerikai civil szervezet ötven vendégmunkással beszélgetett, a teljes jelentés májusban jelenik meg.

stories.title: Caramel a legújabb videójában Nagy Ervinnel közösen buzdít szavazásra
stories.summary: Bár a videó a zenész Instagram-oldalán jelent meg, de a Tisza arculatával zárult.

stories.title: Hann Endre reálisnak tartana egy 74 százalékos választási részvételt, ami rekord lenne
stories.summary: A Medián szerint a Tisza olyan stabil kétharmadot szerezhet április 12-én, amilyenre 2014 óta nem volt példa.

stories.title: Nem indul a választáson Z. Kárpát Dániel
stories.summary: A jobbikos politikus 16 év után távozik a parlamentből, de a közéletet nem hagyja el.

stories.title: A Fidesz idén is a Bálnában várja a választás eredményét
stories.summary: A békepárti kormánypárt elfoglalja vasárnap este a honvédelmi központot.

stories.title: A Tisza szerint Hoppál Péter étellel vásárol szavazatokat, Hoppál szerint ez hazugság, ő ételt kínált egy választónak
stories.summary: Videó készült arról, ahogy Hoppál azt mondja egy választónak, a nevére hivatkozva enni kap valahol, ami a Tisza pécsi jelöltje szerint választási csalás, Hoppál szerint segítség.

stories.title: Hogyan tanultuk meg a kihalás szót? – három történet a pusztításról
stories.summary: Turpeinen könyve újra és újra ugyanarra a kérdésre tér vissza: mit jelent az, hogy egy faj végleg eltűnik és vajon a felismerés, hogy mit tettünk, elegendő-e ahhoz, hogy másképp viselkedjünk a világgal, amelyben élünk.

stories.title: J. D. Vance szombaton Pakisztánban tárgyal az iráni békéről
stories.summary: Donald Trump elképzelése szerint az Egyesült Államok bevételre tenne szert a Hormuzi-szoroson áthaladó hajók után.

stories.title: 15 év börtönre ítélték a dílert, aki eladta Matthew Perrynek a halálát okozó ketamint
stories.summary: Perry négy nappal a halála előtt 25 fiola ketamint vásárolt attól a nőtől, aki az ügy legsúlyosabb ítéletét kapta.

stories.title: Tíz éve még hemzsegtek a békák Farmoson, idén kevesebb mint százat mentettek meg
stories.summary: A Tápió Natúrpark gyászolja a helyi békapopulációt.

stories.title: Ukraine: Several killed as Russia strikes bus, other targets
stories.summary: Meanwhile, Ukrainian drone strikes on Russia and Russian-occupied regions killed at least five, including a 12-year-old boy. Ukraine has been pushing for a ceasefire during the Orthodox Easter, which is later this week.

stories.title: Fake Australian, Chinese and Brazilian police stations: BBC goes inside a seized scam compound
stories.summary: Almost nothing was known about the Royal Hill casino until the Thai military took control of it in December.

stories.title: Trump says 'whole civilisation will die tonight' if Iran ultimatum expires
stories.summary: Previously, the US president has warned that unless Tehran reopened the Strait of Hormuz, the US would unleash what he called the "complete demolition" of Iran's critical infrastructure overnight on Wednesday.

stories.title: France's Sarkozy maintains innocence at Libya election funding trial
stories.summary: Sarkozy was sentenced to five years in prison for his alleged part in a scheme to obtain funds from the government of Libya's leader Muammar Gaddafi in exchange for political and diplomatic favours.

stories.title: 'Only the president knows' what he will do on Iran, White House says in statement
stories.summary: Trump had threatened to target Iranian infrastructure unless Tehran agreed a deal to end the war on Tuesday, saying he was "considering blowing everything up and taking over the oil."

stories.title: Anti-war protesters rally outside US embassy office in Tel Aviv
stories.summary: A small group of anti-war protesters gathered outside the US embassy’s Tel Aviv office, calling for an end to the conflict before police moved to disperse the demonstration.

stories.title: Bangladesh launches measles vaccination drive as child death toll passes 100
stories.summary: UN assists in emergency vaccination drive as country battles worst surge in cases in years amid fall in vaccination rates Bangladesh is battling its worse measles outbreak in years, with more than 100 children dead amid a rise in unvaccinated infants. The government, in partnership with the United Nations, has begun conducting an emergency measles-rubella vaccination drive for children across the country, after more than 900 cases were confirmed since March. Continue reading...

stories.title: Row over ‘virtual gated community’ AI surveillance plan in Toronto neighbourhood
stories.summary: Rosedale residents considering car licence plate-scanning Flock system in bid to tackle property crime A row has broken out in one of Canada’s wealthiest neighbourhoods over plans to use an AI-powered surveillance system to create the country’s first “virtual gated community” to combat surging property crime. Crime rates in Toronto as a whole are dropping but residents of Rosedale have been left on edge by a sustained rise in home invasions, with robbers targeting the tree-lined neighbourhood at

stories.title: Can small nuclear reactors solve Europe's energy woes?
stories.summary: Supporters say modular reactors are key to meeting energy goals, but critics say they're a costly distraction.

stories.title: One in five young Germans plan to leave the country
stories.summary: A recent study shows that as many as one in five Germans between the ages of 14 and 29 are actively thinking about moving to another country — and 40% plan to do so in the long term.

stories.title: How to insulate your household from soaring energy bills
stories.summary: In times of skyrocketing energy prices, record-breaking heat waves and blackouts, producing electricity at home looks more appealing than ever.

stories.title: China is winning one AI race, the US another - but either might pull ahead
stories.summary: Both sides don't want to let their rival dominate. And the competition may yet be transformed further.

stories.title: Centauros: Greece's anti-drone system that changes 'war of drones' at low cost
stories.summary: The anti-drone system, named after a mythical creature symbolising untamed forces of nature, uses electronic warfare technology to detect and neutralise UAVs without munitions.

stories.title: Iran war and energy supply disruptions accelerate South Caucasus collaboration
stories.summary: When it comes to connecting the continents of Asia and Europe, Azerbaijan and Georgia play a vital role in the transit of energy. The leaders of both countries have outlined how they will work together to broaden the Middle Corridor.

stories.title: Chilean woman accused of Pinochet-era kidnaps loses Australia extradition battle
stories.summary: Adriana Rivas moved from Chile to Australia in the 1970s, working there as a nanny and cleaner.
